binaries.title: http.sys - feature-staged double-fetch mitigation in UlGenerateTrailers (UxKir-gated, vulnerable path retained); not a delivered fix

changed_functions.name: UlGenerateTrailers
key_changes: Unpatched: for each 24-byte (0x18) trailer entry, each string pointer (at +0x8 and +0x10) is read from the array once for the probe helper UlProbeAnsiString (0x1c0039148), then RE-READ from the same user-mode memory for the copy helper memmove (0x1c0021200). The length fields are read once and cached; only the pointers are double-fetched.

Patched: reads each pointer once into rcx, stores it to a stack slot (Src), then branches on the feature flag UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm. Flag on (nonzero): validates and copies from the captured stack value (no re-read). Flag off (zero): falls back to the original path and re-reads the pointer from the array for memmove.
The vulnerable double-fetch path is RETAINED in the patched binary (flag-off branch at loc_1C000D125 / loc_1C000D1D1). The change is feature-staged, not an unconditional removal.
The probe helper (UlProbeAnsiString -> UlProbeForRead -> MmUserProbeAddress) and copy helper (memmove) are unchanged; only the calling pattern and the added gate differ.
changed_functions.security_notes: Genuine double-fetch (CWE-367) in the trailer serialization path of the unpatched build: a user-mode trailer string pointer is probed for readability, then re-read from the same user memory for the memmove. The trailer array and strings are read in place (validated via UlProbeForRead / UlProbeAnsiString, not deep-copied), so a second thread in the same process can swap a pointer between probe and re-read. If swapped to a kernel address, memmove copies from it into the response output buffer, giving a local, race-dependent kernel information disclosure. This is NOT remotely triggerable (the pointers are the local server process's own memory). The patched build adds a capture-once mitigation but gates it behind the WIL feature-staging flag UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm (set from Feature_2347446586__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage in UxStartEnvironmentModule) and keeps the original double-fetch in the flag-off branch. The runtime default of the flag is set by feature configuration outside the binary and cannot be determined from the disassembly. Therefore this is a staged/rollback-gated hardening, not an unconditionally delivered security fix.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED double-fetch (cold/outlined section of UlGenerateTrailers):
0x1c002894e: movzx  eax, word ptr [r15+r12*8]   ; read size1 once (cached)
0x1c0028970: mov    rcx, [r15+r12*8+8]          ; FIRST read of ptr1
0x1c0028975: call   UlProbeAnsiString           ; validate ptr1 (-> UlProbeForRead)
0x1c00289a7: mov    rdx, [r15+r12*8+8]          ; SECOND read of ptr1 (double-fetch)
0x1c00289b4: call   memmove                     ; copy from re-read ptr1
0x1c00289e4: movzx  ebx, word ptr [r15+r12*8+2] ; read size2 once
0x1c00289fc: mov    rcx, [r15+r12*8+10h]        ; FIRST read of ptr2
0x1c0028a01: call   UlProbeAnsiString           ; validate ptr2
0x1c0028a21: mov    rdx, [r15+r12*8+10h]        ; SECOND read of ptr2 (double-fetch)
0x1c0028a31: call   memmove                     ; copy from re-read ptr2

PATCHED (feature-gated) capture path:
0x1c000d0cf: mov    rcx, [r13+rax*8+8]          ; read ptr1 ONCE
0x1c000d0d4: mov    [rsp+Src], rcx              ; capture to stack
0x1c000d0e5: cmp    cs:UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm, 0
0x1c000d0ec: jz     loc_1c000d125               ; flag off -> re-read path retained
0x1c000d0ee: call   UlProbeAnsiString           ; (flag on) validate captured ptr1
0x1c000d111: mov    rdx, [rsp+Src]              ; copy source = captured value
0x1c000d119: call   memmove
; flag-off branch keeps the double-fetch:
0x1c000d125: call   UlProbeAnsiString
0x1c000d14d: mov    rdx, [r13+r12*8+8]          ; RE-READ ptr1 from array
0x1c000d155: call   memmove

changed_functions.name: UxQuicConnectionCopyBindings
key_changes: Adds a UxKirWritingAndReadingUm gate around a memmove at 0x1c0047beb. Flag off executes 'mov ecx, ecx' (truncating the destination pointer to 32 bits); flag on skips it.
The gate selects user-mode pointer-width handling for the bindings copy. Operates under an exclusive push lock (ExReleasePushLockExclusiveEx).
changed_functions.security_notes: Companion feature-staging change gated by UxKirWritingAndReadingUm (set from Feature_1389320505__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage). Controls destination pointer width for the QUIC/HTTP3 bindings copy; not a delivered security fix. Note the function header is at 0x1C0047B38.

changed_functions.name: UlpRebuildResponseHeaders
key_changes: Feature-flag branch simplification in the response-rebuild path.

changed_functions.name: UlpCompleteSendToExtensionWorker
key_changes: Restructures case handling in the send-response completion path (references sendresponse.h). Removes feature-flag-gated special handling, making behavior consistent across cases.
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-flag cleanup in the response path, not a direct security fix.

changed_functions.name: UlHkeBuilderCopyFixedHeaders
key_changes: Header-copy helper. Call targets updated to relocated equivalents of the same memmove/helpers; branch condition simplified.
The referenced copy/helper functions are the same operations at different addresses in the patched binary.
changed_functions.security_notes: Behavioral/feature churn in the header serialization helper; the function performs the same operations.

changed_functions.name: UxStartEnvironmentModule
key_changes: Sets WIL feature-staging flags. UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm <- Feature_2347446586__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage; UxKirWritingAndReadingUm <- Feature_1389320505__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage; also UxKirChunkExtHeaderFix and UxKirSetCbtHardeningAsMedium.
Emits WPP trace events for the flag states.
changed_functions.security_notes: Driver initialization / feature-staging configuration. Sets the flag that gates the pointer-capture path in UlGenerateTrailers. Configuration plumbing, not a vulnerability. (No registry value 'AllowNonprivilegedCaptureCred' and no data_1c0099aa* flags are involved; the flags come from WIL feature staging.)

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_IncrementOpportunityInCache
key_changes: WIL feature-usage reporting cache helper. Loop/register churn.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_IncrementUsageInCache
key_changes: WIL feature-usage reporting cache helper. Loop/register churn.

changed_functions.name: wil_details_FeatureReporting_RecordUsageInCache
key_changes: WIL feature-usage reporting cache helper. Loop/register churn; callee names updated to patched equivalents.

security_findings.entry_point: HTTP Server API response send path (HttpSendHttpResponse / HttpSendResponseEntityBody)
call_chain: Response send path -> UlpPrepareHttpResponse (0x1C00DD810) and UlFastSendHttpResponse (0x1C00E21F0)
UlGenerateTrailers probes each trailer string pointer, then (flag-off path) re-reads it for memmove
security_findings.vulnerability_class: Double-fetch / TOCTOU (CWE-367) on user-mode trailer string pointers; consequence is an out-of-bounds/kernel read (CWE-125) if the race is won
security_findings.description: UlGenerateTrailers serializes an array of HTTP trailer entries (each 0x18 bytes: int16_t name_len at +0x0, int16_t value_len at +0x2, qword name_ptr at +0x8, qword value_ptr at +0x10) from an application-supplied buffer into the response output. The array base comes from *(arg2+0x10) and the count from *(arg2+0x8); the array is validated with UlProbeForRead and each string with UlProbeAnsiString (which probes against MmUserProbeAddress), i.e. the strings are read in place from user memory rather than deep-copied. In the unpatched build each string pointer is read for the probe and then RE-READ from the same user memory for the memmove copy (the length is read once and cached). A second thread in the same process can overwrite a pointer field between the probe and the re-read; swapping it to a kernel address makes memmove copy from that address into the response output buffer, which the process can then read. This is a local, race-dependent kernel information disclosure; it is not remotely triggerable because the pointers are supplied by the local server process. The patched build adds a capture-once path but gates it behind UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm and retains the original double-fetch in the flag-off branch, so it is a staged/rollback-gated hardening rather than an unconditionally delivered fix.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1. Local process uses the HTTP Server API (HttpInitialize, HttpCreateHttpHandle/HttpCreateRequestQueue, HttpAddUrl) and obtains a request to respond to. 2. Builds an HTTP_RESPONSE with >=1 trailer whose entry pointer fields initially point to valid user memory. 3. A second thread overwrites a pointer field with a kernel address between the UlProbeAnsiString probe and the memmove re-read. 4. Entry count must be >0 and <0x2710 (check at 0x1C00288DC). 5. The race must be won for at least one entry. Only reachable via the retained flag-off path (UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm == 0).
security_findings.primitive: Local kernel memory read into the response buffer (information disclosure) if the race is won on the flag-off path. Target address is chosen via the swapped pointer; read length is bounded by the entry int16_t length (up to 65535 bytes per entry). Same-process race against the kernel; race window is a few instructions between probe and re-read, so success is probabilistic. Not a self-contained privilege escalation and not remote.
security_findings.evidence:
UNPATCHED (cold/outlined section of UlGenerateTrailers):
0x1c0028970: mov  rcx, [r15+r12*8+8]     ; FIRST read of ptr1
0x1c0028975: call UlProbeAnsiString      ; validate ptr1
0x1c00289a7: mov  rdx, [r15+r12*8+8]     ; SECOND read of ptr1 (double-fetch)
0x1c00289b4: call memmove                ; copy from re-read ptr1
0x1c00289fc: mov  rcx, [r15+r12*8+10h]   ; FIRST read of ptr2
0x1c0028a01: call UlProbeAnsiString      ; validate ptr2
0x1c0028a21: mov  rdx, [r15+r12*8+10h]   ; SECOND read of ptr2 (double-fetch)
0x1c0028a31: call memmove                ; copy from re-read ptr2

PATCHED feature-gated path:
0x1c000d0cf: mov  rcx, [r13+rax*8+8]     ; read ptr1 ONCE
0x1c000d0d4: mov  [rsp+Src], rcx         ; capture to stack
0x1c000d0e5: cmp  cs:UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm, 0
0x1c000d0ec: jz   loc_1c000d125          ; flag off -> re-read path retained
0x1c000d111: mov  rdx, [rsp+Src]         ; (flag on) copy from captured value
0x1c000d119: call memmove
0x1c000d14d: mov  rdx, [r13+r12*8+8]     ; (flag off) RE-READ ptr1 from array
0x1c000d155: call memmove
security_findings.assembly:
; UlGenerateTrailers @ 0x1C000CF94 - key unpatched double-fetch sites:
0x1c002894e: movzx  eax, word ptr [r15+r12*8]   ; read size1 once (cached)
0x1c0028970: mov    rcx, [r15+r12*8+8]          ; FIRST read of ptr1
0x1c0028975: call   UlProbeAnsiString           ; validate ptr1 (-> UlProbeForRead -> MmUserProbeAddress)
0x1c00289a7: mov    rdx, [r15+r12*8+8]          ; SECOND read of ptr1 (double-fetch)
0x1c00289b4: call   memmove                     ; copy from re-read ptr1
0x1c00289e4: movzx  ebx, word ptr [r15+r12*8+2] ; read size2 once
0x1c00289fc: mov    rcx, [r15+r12*8+10h]        ; FIRST read of ptr2
0x1c0028a01: call   UlProbeAnsiString           ; validate ptr2
0x1c0028a21: mov    rdx, [r15+r12*8+10h]        ; SECOND read of ptr2 (double-fetch)
0x1c0028a31: call   memmove                     ; copy from re-read ptr2

breakpoints.why: call UlProbeAnsiString validating ptr1. rcx = source pointer from the user-mode trailer array. Record this value; the race window begins when this returns.
mov rdx, [r15+r12*8+8] - the second read of ptr1 before the copy. If rdx differs from the value validated at 0x1C0028975, the pointer changed between probe and copy (race won).
Copy helper. When called from UlGenerateTrailers, rdx = source (the re-read pointer), rcx = destination in the response buffer, r8 = length.
mov rdx, [r15+r12*8+10h] - the second read of the value pointer ptr2, the equivalent double-fetch site for the value string.

watch: r15 = base of the user-mode trailer array
r12*8 = byte offset of the current entry (index * 0x18)
At memmove entry: rdx should match the pointer validated by the prior UlProbeAnsiString call; if they differ the race was won
key_offsets: 0x1c0028970: mov rcx, [r15+r12*8+8] - first read of name pointer (pre-probe)
0x1c0028975: call UlProbeAnsiString - probe of name pointer
0x1c00289a7: mov rdx, [r15+r12*8+8] - second read of name pointer (double-fetch)
0x1c00289b4: call memmove - copy using re-read name pointer
0x1c00289fc: mov rcx, [r15+r12*8+10h] - first read of value pointer
0x1c0028a01: call UlProbeAnsiString - probe of value pointer
0x1c0028a21: mov rdx, [r15+r12*8+10h] - second read of value pointer (double-fetch)
0x1c0028a31: call memmove - copy using re-read value pointer
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Local process only. Initialize the HTTP Server API (HttpInitialize, HttpCreateHttpHandle/HttpCreateRequestQueue, HttpAddUrl), obtain a request, build an HTTP_RESPONSE with a trailer whose entry pointer fields (name at +0x8, value at +0x10) initially point to valid user memory, spawn a second thread that overwrites a pointer field with a kernel address, then call HttpSendHttpResponse. Reachable only via the retained flag-off path (UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm == 0).
debugging_aids.expected_observation: When the race is won on the flag-off path, at the memmove site rdx (source) holds a kernel address rather than the user-mode pointer validated by UlProbeAnsiString, and the copy places kernel bytes into the response buffer readable by the local process. An invalid kernel source address faults during memmove.
debugging_aids.notes: The race window is a few instructions between the probe at 0x1c0028975 and the re-read at 0x1c00289a7 (similarly for the value pointer). Practical exploitation is probabilistic and same-process/local. In the patched binary this path is only reached when UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm is zero; when the flag is set the pointer is captured once and the race is closed. The flag default is set by feature staging outside the binary.

summary: The unpatched build contains a genuine double-fetch (CWE-367) in UlGenerateTrailers (0x1C000CF94): each HTTP trailer string pointer is probed via UlProbeAnsiString (-> UlProbeForRead -> MmUserProbeAddress) and then re-read from the same user-mode memory for the memmove copy (the length is single-fetched). Because the trailer array and strings are read in place, a second thread in the same process can swap a pointer between probe and re-read to make memmove copy from a kernel address into the response buffer, a local, race-dependent kernel information disclosure (not remote). The patched build does NOT remove this unconditionally: it adds a capture-once path gated behind the WIL feature-staging flag UxKirHttpReadingTrailersFromUm (set from Feature_2347446586__private_IsEnabledDeviceUsage in UxStartEnvironmentModule) and retains the original double-fetch in the flag-off branch. This is a staged/rollback-gated hardening, not an unconditionally delivered security fix, and the flag's runtime default is set outside the binary. Remaining diff entries are companion feature-staging plumbing (UxQuicConnectionCopyBindings gated by UxKirWritingAndReadingUm; UxStartEnvironmentModule flag setup) and WIL feature-reporting/telemetry churn (wil_details_FeatureReporting_*). Report corrections: real function names replace the address labels; the copy helper is memmove (0x1C0021200) not memcpy and the probe helper is UlProbeAnsiString (0x1C0039148); the invented data_1c0099aa* flags and the 'AllowNonprivilegedCaptureCred' registry claim are removed; the fabricated remote/network leak, KASLR-defeat and LPE-chaining narrative is removed; 0x1C0028A91 is part of UlGenerateTrailers, not a separate function; severity lowered from high to low to reflect the local, race-dependent, feature-gated nature.
